title: Heroes & Comics
subtitle: Superhero films and broader comic-book adaptations.

sections.name: I. Marvel — MCU
sections.desc: Marvel Studios proper. Iron Man through current.

items.title: Iron Man
items.pitch: Tony Stark builds a powered exoskeleton to escape captivity, then rebrands as a high-tech vigilante. Foundational MCU — the moment the modern superhero machine got its tone.

items.title: The Avengers
items.pitch: First major team-up of Iron Man, Cap, Thor, Hulk, Black Widow, and Hawkeye against Loki and a Chitauri invasion of New York. The crossover that proved the model would scale.

items.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
items.pitch: Star-Lord, Gamora, Drax, Rocket, and Groot reluctantly team to save the universe — set to a 70s mixtape that became as iconic as the film.

items.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier
items.pitch: Steve Rogers vs. a brainwashed Bucky Barnes inside a 70s political-thriller skeleton dressed as superhero film. Series high point for many.

items.title: Doctor Strange
items.pitch: Arrogant neurosurgeon loses his hands, finds the Mystic Arts. Inception-style reality-folding visuals are the headline.

items.title: Black Panther
items.pitch: T'Challa returns home to Wakanda to claim the throne, faces Killmonger — a villain whose grievance is harder to dismiss than usual. Cultural moment, Best Picture nominee.

items.title: Avengers: Infinity War
items.pitch: Thanos collects the Infinity Stones, the Avengers can't stop him, half of all life is wiped out. The cliffhanger that made the audience gasp.

items.title: Avengers: Endgame
items.pitch: Time-heist conclusion to the Infinity Saga. Ten-year payoff for the team-up era.

sections.name: II. Marvel — Non-MCU
sections.desc: Fox X-Men/FF era, Sony Spider-Verse, Sony alt-Marvel, Spider-Man trilogies.

items.title: X-Men
items.pitch: Bryan Singer launches the modern superhero film. Mutants vs. Magneto's Brotherhood, Wolverine introduced to mainstream audiences.

items.title: X2: X-Men United
items.pitch: Sequel that elevates the X-Men trilogy — Stryker, Nightcrawler's White House attack opening, the Jean Grey sacrifice.

items.title: Spider-Man
items.pitch: Sam Raimi's earnest, comic-book-coded Peter Parker origin. Defining Spider-Man performance for a generation.

items.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
items.pitch: Miles Morales meets every Spider-Person across the multiverse. Animation that broke open what the genre could look like.

items.title: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
items.pitch: Spider-Verse Part 2. Even more ambitious art-direction — each universe rendered in its own visual language. Cliffhanger ending.

items.title: Deadpool
items.pitch: Wade Wilson breaks the fourth wall, the rating, and the genre. The R-rated superhero film that proved there was an audience for it.

items.title: Logan
items.pitch: Western-coded Wolverine endgame. Aged Logan, Professor X with seizures, X-23 — the rare superhero film willing to be a real elegy.

sections.name: III. DC
sections.desc: DC Extended Universe, Snyderverse, current DCU, prior eras.

items.title: The Dark Knight
items.pitch: Heath Ledger's Joker vs. Bale's Batman in a post-9/11 crime epic that happens to wear a cowl. Genre-redefining performance.

items.title: Joker
items.pitch: Phoenix's Arthur Fleck descends into the title character, Scorsese-by-way-of-Gotham. Best Actor win, polarizing reception.

items.title: Wonder Woman
items.pitch: Diana of Themyscira enters WWI. The first DCEU film to land critically; Gadot anchors a sincere, optimistic origin.

items.title: The Batman
items.pitch: Reeves's Year-Two Batman as detective. Pattinson's brooding Bruce, Riddler as Zodiac stand-in, Nirvana on the soundtrack.

items.title: Aquaman
items.pitch: Wan's underwater fantasy, full Saturday-morning operatic energy. Momoa as Arthur Curry, Patrick Wilson chewing scenery.

items.title: The Suicide Squad
items.pitch: Gunn's R-rated reboot. Bloater, Polka-Dot Man, Ratcatcher 2 vs. Starro the Conqueror. The 2016 film disowned and replaced.

items.title: Joker: Folie à Deux
items.pitch: Musical sequel to Joker. Phoenix and Lady Gaga in a folie-à-deux courtroom-and-Arkham two-hander built on jukebox standards. Polarizing.

sections.name: IV. Indie / Other Publishers
sections.desc: Hellboy, Dredd, Kick-Ass, Image, Dark Horse, etc.

items.title: Watchmen
items.pitch: Snyder's faithful-to-a-fault adaptation of Moore/Gibbons. Doctor Manhattan on Mars, Rorschach's journal, the squid replaced by a blue alternate.

items.title: V for Vendetta
items.pitch: Wachowski-scripted Alan Moore adaptation. Dystopian future Britain, masked anarchist V, Natalie Portman radicalized into action.

items.title: Kick-Ass
items.pitch: Mark Millar adaptation about a normal kid who decides to be a superhero. Hit-Girl's introduction is the cultural memory.

items.title: Hellboy
items.pitch: Del Toro's Mike Mignola adaptation. Ron Perlman as the demon-with-a-day-job, Lovecraftian texture, hand-built creature work.

sections.name: V. Deconstructive
sections.desc: Watchmen, Joker, the genre interrogating itself.

sections.name: VI. Cosmic
sections.desc: Guardians of the Galaxy, Eternals, Captain Marvel, the space-tier of the genre.

items.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
items.pitch: Gunn's Guardians sendoff. Rocket's origin, the High Evolutionary as a cruel god — darker and more felt than the prior two.

items.title: Deadpool & Wolverine
items.pitch: Wade and Logan brought into the MCU. Cameo-heavy multiverse romp, leans on audience nostalgia for Fox-era X-Men.

items.title: Thunderbolts*
items.pitch: MCU's anti-team — Yelena, Bucky, US Agent, Ghost, Red Guardian. Quieter, character-driven take after Phase 4 fatigue.

sections.name: IX. Non-Comic Super-Powered
sections.desc: Unbreakable, Chronicle, The Incredibles, Brightburn — superpowers without the comic source.

items.title: The Incredibles
items.pitch: Pixar superhero family. Bird's argument about excellence, suburbia, and capes — dressed as a Saturday cartoon.

items.title: Unbreakable
items.pitch: Shyamalan's quiet superhero origin. David Dunn, Mr. Glass, the comic-book mythology read as elegy. The original of the Eastrail 177 trilogy.

items.title: Chronicle
items.pitch: Found-footage origin film. Three teens get powers; one of them is Andrew. Concise, mean, and effective.

items.title: Brightburn
items.pitch: What if Superman were evil? Horror-coded answer. Gunn-produced; brutal in places, deliberate concept.

items.title: Glass
items.pitch: Eastrail 177 trilogy capper. Dunn, Mr. Glass, and Kevin's many personalities all in the same psychiatric hospital.

sections.name: Z. Plex History (Auto-Promoted)
sections.desc: Auto-promoted from Plex viewing history. Items also represented in proper sections above.

items.title: Kingsman: The Golden Circle
items.pitch: Kingsman sequel — Eggsy, Statesman crossover, Julianne Moore's candy-coated villain.

items.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
items.pitch: Alan Moore adaptation universally hated by Moore. Sean Connery's last film.

items.title: The New Mutants
items.pitch: Fox X-Men spinoff buried by Disney transition. Horror-coded mutant teens.

items.title: X-Men: Apocalypse
items.pitch: Singer's Apocalypse-era X-Men. Generally regarded as the weakest of the modern X-Men trilogy.

items.title: X-Men: Dark Phoenix
items.pitch: Disjointed Dark Phoenix adaptation. Final pre-Disney Fox X-Men film.

items.title: X-Men: First Class
items.pitch: Vaughn-directed 60s-set X-Men prequel. McAvoy/Fassbender as Charles/Erik. Critical reset for the Fox era.
